chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّدِّ إِلَى الْكِتَابِ وَ السُّنَّةِ وَ أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ شَيْ‏ءٌ مِنَ الْحَلَالِ وَ الْحَرَامِ وَ جَمِيعِ مَا يَحْتَاجُ النَّاسُ إِلَيْهِ إِلَّا وَ قَدْ جَاءَ فِيهِ كِتَابٌ أَوْ سُنَّةٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 20 – The referring to the Book and the Sunnah, and that there is nothing from the Permissible and the Prohibition and the entirety of what the people would be need to except that the Book or Sunnah has come with it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/204)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/204)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا مِنْ أَمْرٍ يَخْتَلِفُ فِيهِ اثْنَانِ إِلَّا وَ لَهُ أَصْلٌ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ لَكِنْ لَا تَبْلُغُهُ عُقُولُ الرِّجَالِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Sa’alba Bin Maymoun, from the one who narrated it, from Al Moalla Bin Khunays who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is none from a matter regarding which two (people) differ except for it would have its origin in the Book of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, but the intellects of the men do not reach it’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from Hammad from ’Abdallah ibn Sinan from abu al-Jarud who has said the following. “Abu Ja‘far (a.s.) has said, ‘When you speak of anything ask me for its law in the book of Allah. He also has said in his Hadith, ‘The holy Prophet prohibited much squabbling and brawling, spoiling of property and much questionings.’” A person then asked, “O descendent of the holy Prophet, where is this in the holy Quran?” The Imam (a.s.) replied, “Allah, the Most Glorious, the Most Majestic, says, ‘There is nothing good in much of their secret talks except for that which is for charity, justice, or for reconciliation among people to . [4:114] Do not give to people weak of understanding your property for which God has made you to supervise . [4:5] Believers, do not ask about things which, if revealed to you, would disappoint you . [5:101]”
